title: Find First Duplicate in Sorted Array

statement.markdown: Given a sorted array of integers and a target number, find the first duplicate in the array. If no duplicate is found, return -1. The array is sorted in ascending order.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a string containing a sorted array of integers and a target number separated by a space, e.g. "1 2 3 4 5 2"
statement.outputFormat: Output will be an integer representing the first duplicate found in the array, or -1 if no duplicate is found.
statement.notes: The array is sorted in ascending order. Time complexity should be O(n).

examples.explanation: The first duplicate in the array is 2.
No duplicate is found in the array.
The first duplicate in the array is 2.

hints.text: Iterate through the array to find the first duplicate.

editorial.title: Editorial: Find First Duplicate in Sorted Array
editorial.intuition: The problem can be solved by iterating through the array and keeping track of the previous element.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Iterate through the array and check for every element if it exists in the rest of the array
editorial.betterSolution: Use a hash set to keep track of the elements seen so far
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterate through the array and check if the current element is equal to the previous element. If it is, return the current element. Otherwise, return -1.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution is correct because it checks every element in the array and returns the first duplicate found.
commonMistakes: Not handling the case where the array is empty
